perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the above restrictions shall not apply to the following: (i) Information that is publicly available at the time of disclosure (through no act of Holdings of any of its Representatives); (ii) Information that is disclosed to Holdings by a third party that did not disclose it in violation of a duty of confidentiality; (iii) Information that was known to Holdings before such Information was provided to it or its Representatives by or on behalf of Big Sky; (iv) disclosures that are required to be made by Holdings or any of its Representatives under legal process by subpoena or other court order or other applicable laws or regulations (provided, however, that Holdings makes reasonable efforts to provide copies of such Information to, or informs Big Sky before disclosure), or that are requested by Big Sky.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the above restrictions shall not apply to Information that is publicly available at the time of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exceptions (ii), (iii), and (iv) regarding confidential information received from third parties, pre-existing knowledge, and legal disclosures, the contract now creates an internal contradiction because Holdings is now restricted from disclosing information that it legally has to under legal process by subpoena or other court order or other applicable laws or regulations. This creates uncertainty about Holdings's obligations when faced with legal mandates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the prior written consent of Big Sky, neither Holdings nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Information or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains exceptions to the confidentiality restrictions, including disclosures required by legal process. The changed text removes these exceptions, meaning Holdings is now restricted from disclosing information it is legally obligated to disclose. This directly contradicts the scenario where legal disclosure is required, creating a clear conflict with the stated confidentiality obligations in the contradicted text. The change creates a contradiction between the requirement to not disclose information and the legal obligation to disclose, leading to uncertainty about Holdings' obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Without the prior written consent of Big Sky, neither Holdings nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Information or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction, including the status thereof. Likewise, without the prior written consent of Holdings, neither Big Sky nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Information or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the prior written consent of Big Sky, Holdings shall not disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Information or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction, including the status thereof. Likewise, without the prior written consent of Holdings, Big Sky shall not disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Information or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'nor its Representatives', the text appears to imply that the restrictions no longer apply to the Representatives of Holdings and Big Sky, allowing Representatives to disclose the Information, even though the agreement is stating that Holdings and Big Sky should not, which creates a loophole.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of Big Sky's disclosure to Holdings of the Information, Holdings agrees that it will keep the Information confidential and that the Information will not, without the prior written consent of Big Sky, be disclosed by Holdings or by its officers, directors, employees, agents or representatives (collectively, "Representatives"), in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and shall not be used by Holdings or by its Representatives, other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited both Holdings/Big Sky 'nor its Representatives' from disclosing information. The changed text removes 'nor its Representatives', which, according to the Perturbation Explanation, implies that the Representatives of Holdings and Big Sky are no longer restricted from disclosing the Information. This creates a direct contradiction with the Contradicted Text, which explicitly states that 'Holdings or by its officers, directors, employees, agents or representatives (collectively, "Representatives"), in any manner whatsoever' will not disclose the Information. This falls under the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as essential language has been removed creating a contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Big Sky and Holdings agree that, at the conclusion of their review of the Information, or within three business days of the other's request, all copies of the Information, in any form whatsoever, will be delivered by Holdings and its Representatives to Big Sky.
perturbation.changed_text: Big Sky and Holdings agree that, at the conclusion of their review of the Information, or within three business days of the other's request, all copies of the Information will be delivered by Holdings to Big Sky.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and its Representatives' from the delivery clause, the agreement now only obligates Holdings (and not its Representatives) to return the Information. This creates a loophole, as the Representatives are still in posession of the Information, which implies they do not have to deliver back the information, which creates a contradiction with the original intention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of Big Sky's disclosure to Holdings of the Information, Holdings agrees that it will keep the Information confidential and that the Information will not, without the prior written consent of Big Sky, be disclosed by Holdings or by its officers, directors, employees, agents or representatives (collectively, "Representatives"), in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and shall not be used by Holdings or by its Representatives, other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'and its Representatives' from the delivery clause, meaning only Holdings is obligated to return information. The contradicted text states that both Holdings and its Representatives will keep the information confidential. This creates a loophole where the Representatives are not explicitly required to return the Information, which contradicts the implied intention of the agreement regarding confidentiality and return of information, fitting the 'Omissions' definition of in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During such time as the parties are involved in discussions or negotiations concerning the Transaction and for a period of at least 60 days after conclusion thereof, neither party shall engage in any transactions whatsoever regarding the common stock of the other, whether publicly traded or privately held, registered or nonregistered, including, but not limited to, buying, selling, taking or granting options in or making or receiving offers or tender offers to buy, sell, trade, or exchange any stock of the other, without the express written consent of the other and without first fully complying with all applicable securities laws and regulations and the rules of all exchanges upon which the stock of each respective party is traded, provided however, that the foregoing shall not be construed to limit the right of either party to invest in mutual funds or other commercially available investment vehicles that may acquire the stock of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: During such time as the parties are involved in discussions or negotiations concerning the Transaction and for a period of at least 60 days after conclusion thereof, neither party shall engage in any transactions whatsoever regarding the common stock of the other, whether publicly traded or privately held, registered or nonregistered, without the express written consent of the other, provided however, that the foregoing shall not be construed to limit the right of either party to invest in mutual funds or other commercially available investment vehicles that may acquire the stock of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'including, but not limited to, buying, selling, taking or granting options in or making or receiving offers or tender offers to buy, sell, trade, or exchange any stock of the other and without first fully complying with all applicable securities laws and regulations and the rules of all exchanges upon which the stock of each respective party is traded', the section now prohibits engagement in 'any transactions whatsoever' but doesn't define or enumerate what those transactions are. This creates ambiguity and internal contradiction since without the express listing, it is uncertain what transcactions are being referred, even though the original clause was aiming to include all transactions. The parties involved are required to not engage, but not engaging in what exactly?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that it has been informed that the other party is a publicly-traded corporation and that the Information and the Transaction must be secured from any use or dissemination until such time as proper disclosure can be provided to shareholders and the securities markets in order to protect the interests of shareholders of each party and so that the rules and statutes pertaining to information about and trading in publicly held securities can be followed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes specific examples of 'transactions' related to stock, but the contradicted text discusses securing the 'Transaction' from use or dissemination to protect shareholders and follow securities laws. Since the changed text's removal of examples creates ambiguity about what 'any transactions whatsoever' encompasses, it weakens the enforceability and clarity of the agreement with respect to the protection of shareholders and adherence to securities laws as outlined in the contradicted text. The original text listed items such as 'buying, selling, taking or granting options' but the change removed all of that. This means that the parties are required to not engage in 'any transactions whatsoever', which is not clearly defined. This creates a strong contradiction.
